$note: Captured snapshot of the COMPARED forecast/intelligence product for the azimuth benchmark (synthesis/benchmark.py). Quoted by product + capture date; NOT surfaced as an azimuth channel and NOT an L1 source. Refresh with pull_benchmark_foils.py.

foil.title: Energy repricing risk from Black Sea maritime disruption state
foil.scenario_short: Black Sea maritime disruption state, with 69% pressure and 72% state confirmation, will drive energy repricing through gas supply stress over the next 30 days, transmitted via 1 edge and reinforced by 8 urgent...
foil.perspective_strategic: The 69% pressure and 72% state confirmation signals indicate that gas supply stress is the primary transmission mechanism, with 8 urgent critical signals reinforcing energy repricing, but the thin public reporting limits confidence in the path.
foil.perspective_contrarian: The lack of directly matched headlines attached to Black Sea, as noted in the counter-evidence, could prevent confirmation of the escalation path, allowing the 75% confidence level to cap the forecast and keep it below the 61% baseline.
foil.actor_lens: Commodity traders: Price whether stress in Black Sea becomes durable over the 30d. Rebalance positions if the probability path moves away from 61%.
foil.top_signal: Black Sea maritime disruption state is feeding energy repricing through gas supply stress, with 69% pressure and 72% state confirmation

seismic-infrastructure.absence_reason: No product publishes a seismic forecast — earthquakes are not predictable, so there is no forecast/intel alternative to azimuth's observed USGS record. This is the honest inverse: for an observed natural-hazard fact, the sourced record IS the only trustworthy source.
